canonical_question: How do I secure the software supply chain (npm, pip)?
aliases: software supply chain security best practices

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: SLSA v1.0 released 2023-04; npm provenance GA 2023-04; PyPI Trusted Publishers GA 2023-04; OWASP Top 10 2025 elevated supply chain to A03

constraints: NEVER install packages without verifying integrity -- use lockfiles with hashes
NEVER run npm install in CI -- use npm ci which enforces lockfile integrity
NEVER trust package lifecycle scripts by default -- disable with --ignore-scripts
Private package names MUST be scoped (@org/pkg) or reserved in public registries
SBOM generation is required by US Executive Order 14028 for federal software suppliers

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need to prevent XSS or injection attacks in your own code
Need container image security scanning (not signing/provenance)
Need secrets management, not dependency security

inputs_needed.question: Which package manager are you using?
What is your primary supply chain concern?
